{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10855536]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kushida-jinja
櫛田神社 | image = Kushidajinjafukuoka01.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|34.7|N|130|24|37.7|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | founded_by = | established = 757 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-41, Kamikawabata-machi, [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]]
[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] 812-0026 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kushida Shrine | image = [[File:Kushidajinjafukuoka01.jpg|250px]]
main hall | location = 1-41 Kamikawabatamachi, Hakata-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | ISO = JP-40 | type degrees = 33 | type minutes = 35 | type seconds = 34.641 | longitude degrees = 130 | longitude minutes = 24 | longitude seconds = 37.814 | Label Position = left | festival Deities = [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami|Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami)
[[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami) | Shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) | festival = [[:en:May 3|May 3]]–[[:en:May 4|May 4]] (Hakata Dontaku)
[[:en:July 1|July 1]]–[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Cave Tombvi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}}

Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Cave Tombvi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}}

[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] 812-0026 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kushida Shrine | image = [[File:Kushidajinjafukuoka01.jpg|250px]]
main hall | location = 1-41 Kamikawabatamachi, Hakata-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] | ISO = JP-40 | type degrees = 33 | type minutes = 35 | type seconds = 34.641 | longitude degrees = 130 | longitude minutes = 24 | longitude seconds = 37.814 | Label Position = left | festival Deities = [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami|Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami)
[[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami) | Shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) | festival = [[:en:May 3|May 3]]–[[:en:May 4|May 4]] (Hakata Dontaku)
[[:en:July 1|July 1]]–[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Cave Tombvi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}}

However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - [[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami) | Shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) | festival = [[:en:May 3|May 3]]–[[:en:May 4|May 4]] (Hakata Dontaku)
[[:en:July 1|July 1]]–[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Cave Tombvi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}}

Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Cave Tombvi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami) | Shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) | festival = [[:en:May 3|May 3]]–[[:en:May 4|May 4]] (Hakata Dontaku)
[[:en:July 1|July 1]]–[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Cave Tombvi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - [[:en:July 1|July 1]]–[[:en:July 15|July 15]] (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher=[[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general [[:en:Chinjusha|Chinjusha]] (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the [[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] 1 (757) when the deity from [[:en:Kushida |Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka]] was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in [[:en:Kanzaki|Kanzaki]], [[:en:Hizen Province|Hizen Province]] (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in [[:en:1965|1965]] and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in [[:en:1979|1979]], both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]],'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in [[:en:1587|1587]] (Tensho 15), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in [[:en:1868|1868]] (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by [[:en:Jingū-ji|Jingū-ji]] belonging to [[:en:Tōchō-ji|Tōchō-ji]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. In [[:en:1891|1891]] (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since [[:en:1960|1960]] (Showa 35), [[:en:Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting]] (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in [[:en:2018|2018]] (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In [[:en:1955|1955]] (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] [[:en:Kushida |Kushida ]] - 1 minute walk (140m). * [[:en:Fukuoka City Subway|Fukuoka City Subway]] [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] [[:en:Gion Station (Fukuoka)|Gion Station]] - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, [[:en:1968|1968]], p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], [[:en:1979|1979]], pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *[[:en:List of Shinto shrines|List of Shinto shrines]] *[[:en:Hakata Gion Yamakasa|Hakata Gion Yamakasa]] *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Cave Tombvi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
