[[ja:櫛田神社]][[ko:구시다 신사]] [[ja:櫛田神社]][[ko:구시다 신사]] [[ja:櫛田神社]][[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kushida-jinja
櫛田神社 | image = Kushidajinjafukuoka01.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'', or main shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|34.7|N|130|24|37.7|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] | founded_by = | established = 757 | date_destroyed = | location = 1-41, Kamikawabata-machi, {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1330839|lt=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}}
{{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} 812-0026 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kushida Shrine | image = [[File:Kushidajinjafukuoka01.jpg|250px]]
main hall | location = 1-41 Kamikawabatamachi, Hakata-ku, [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} | ISO = JP-40 | type degrees = 33 | type minutes = 35 | type seconds = 34.641 | longitude degrees = 130 | longitude minutes = 24 | longitude seconds = 37.814 | Label Position = left | festival Deities = [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami|Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami)
[[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami) | Shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1 (757) | festival = {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|qid=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}}–{{ill|May 4|en|May 4|qid=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}} (Hakata Dontaku)
{{ill|July 1|en|July 1|qid=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}–{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1330839|lt=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka|1=Kushida}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1 (757) when the deity from {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka|1=Kushida}} was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō|1=Tengyō}} 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in {{ill|Kanzaki|en|Kanzaki|qid=Q1728232|lt=Kanzaki|1=Kanzaki}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|qid=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979|1=1979}}, both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}},'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in {{ill|1587|en|1587|qid=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} (Tensho 15), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} belonging to {{ill|Tōchō-ji|en|Tōchō-ji|qid=Q3547067|lt=Tōchō-ji|1=Tōchō-ji}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. In {{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}} (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since {{ill|1960|en|1960|qid=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Showa 35), {{ill|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|en|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|qid=Q843686|lt=Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|1=Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting}} (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In {{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|en|Fukuoka City Subway|qid=Q289242|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|1=Fukuoka City Subway}} [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida |1=Kushida}} - 1 minute walk (140m). * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|en|Fukuoka City Subway|qid=Q289242|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|1=Fukuoka City Subway}} [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] {{ill|Gion Station (Fukuoka)|en|Gion Station (Fukuoka)|qid=Q4357517|lt=Gion Station|1=Gion Station (Fukuoka)}} - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, {{ill|1968|en|1968|qid=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}, p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979|1=1979}}, pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Yokoanabovi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}} [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami) | Shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1 (757) | festival = {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|qid=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}}–{{ill|May 4|en|May 4|qid=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}} (Hakata Dontaku)
{{ill|July 1|en|July 1|qid=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}–{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1330839|lt=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka|1=Kushida}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1 (757) when the deity from {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka|1=Kushida}} was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō|1=Tengyō}} 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in {{ill|Kanzaki|en|Kanzaki|qid=Q1728232|lt=Kanzaki|1=Kanzaki}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|qid=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979|1=1979}}, both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}},'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in {{ill|1587|en|1587|qid=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} (Tensho 15), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} belonging to {{ill|Tōchō-ji|en|Tōchō-ji|qid=Q3547067|lt=Tōchō-ji|1=Tōchō-ji}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. In {{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}} (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since {{ill|1960|en|1960|qid=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Showa 35), {{ill|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|en|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|qid=Q843686|lt=Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|1=Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting}} (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In {{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree - Nishinippon Photo Library|url=http://c.nishinippon.co.jp/photolibrary/cat672/post_1691.php|website=c.nishinippon.co.jp|accessdate=2020-04-08|language=ja}}. == Gallery == File:Fukuoka Kushida kazariyama.jpg|Decorated Yamakasa float in the precincts File:Fukuoka kushida ginnan.jpg|The 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree, Kushida no Ginnan File:20100720 Fukuoka Kushida 3614.jpg|Celestial direction chart on the ceiling of the tower gate File:Fukuoka Kushida Setsubun 2011.jpg|Giant Otafuku mask displayed around Setsubun File:Kushida Shrine02.jpg|Ema (votive plaques) in the precincts File:Kushida Shrine04.jpg|Chōzuya (purification pavilion) in the precincts File:Kushida shrine in Hakata wharf.jpg|Hama-miya at Hakata Wharf == Transportation == * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|en|Fukuoka City Subway|qid=Q289242|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|1=Fukuoka City Subway}} [[Fukuoka City Subway Nanakuma Line|Nanakuma Line]] {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida |1=Kushida}} - 1 minute walk (140m). * {{ill|Fukuoka City Subway|en|Fukuoka City Subway|qid=Q289242|lt=Fukuoka City Subway|1=Fukuoka City Subway}} [[Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line|Kūkō Line]] {{ill|Gion Station (Fukuoka)|en|Gion Station (Fukuoka)|qid=Q4357517|lt=Gion Station|1=Gion Station (Fukuoka)}} - 5 minutes walk (400m). * Fukuoka City Subway Kūkō Line and [[Fukuoka City Subway Hakozaki Line|Hakozaki Line]] [[Nakasu-Kawabata Station]] - 8 minutes walk (660m). * [[Nishitetsu Bus]] - Get off at "Canal City Hakata-mae" or "Canal East Building-mae" bus stop. * [[Fukuoka Expressway Ring Road]] [[Gofukumachi Interchange]] - 1.2km. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Books == {{Citation style|date=March 2018|section=1}} * Sukenari Azusa, Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, ''Shinto Dictionary,'' Jinja Shinpōsha, {{ill|1968|en|1968|qid=Q2644|lt=1968|1=1968}}, p. 26. * Eiji Shirai, Masanori Toki, editors, ''Shrine Dictionary,'' [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979|1=1979}}, pp. 123-124. == External Links == {{commonscat|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} * [https://www.hakatayamakasa.com/61866.html Kushida Shrine] - Hakata Gion Yamakasa official website {{Shinto temples}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} *[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== Official website: http://hakatanomiryoku.com/ {{Commons Category:kk]] [[Category-inline|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} *[http://yokanavi.com/eg/landmark/index/135 Yokoanabovi.com] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|櫛田神社|version=87392250|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Kushida Shrine]] [[ja:櫛田神社]] [[ja:Kushida Shrine]] [[ko:구시다 신사]] {{Commons category|Kushida-jinja, Fukuoka}} [[fr:Kushida-jinja]] [[ja:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] [[ko:구시다 신사 (후쿠오카시)]] [[ru:Кусида-дзиндзя]] [[zh:櫛田神社 (福岡市)]] {{wikidata link|Q10855536}} [[Category:Shinto shrines by tradition]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Amaterasu]]
[[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami) | Shrine Rank = Former [[Prefectural Shrine]], [[Beppyo Shrine]] | established = {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1 (757) | festival = {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|qid=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}}–{{ill|May 4|en|May 4|qid=Q2549|lt=May 4|1=May 4}} (Hakata Dontaku)
{{ill|July 1|en|July 1|qid=Q2700|lt=July 1|1=July 1}}–{{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}} (Hakata Gion Yamakasa) | map = Fukuoka city }} {{Nihongo|'''Kushida-jinja'''|櫛田神社}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1330839|lt=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Dedicated to [[Amaterasu]] and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], it is said to have been founded in 757. The {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} [[Japanese festivals|festival]] is centred on the shrine.{{cite web |url=http://www.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/showcase/english/theme/db_object_37.html |title=Kushida Shrine |publisher={{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} |accessdate=24 March 2011}} ==Overview== '''Kushida Shrine''' (くしだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamikawabatamachi, [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The former [[shrine Rank]] was [[Prefectural Shrine]]. It is situated in the old town area of Hakata and is affectionately known by locals as "'''Okushida-san'''." Its formal name is '''Hakata Sōchinshu Kushida Shrine''' (はかたそうちんじゅくしだじんじゃ). == Overview == The shrine has long been revered as the [[Ujigami]] and general {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|qid=Q13479654|lt=Chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} (guardian deity) of Hakata. It hosts festivals such as the {{ill|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|en|Hakata Gion Yamakasa|qid=Q1749262|lt=Hakata Gion Yamakasa|1=Hakata Gion Yamakasa}} in July and the [[Hakata Okunchi]] in October. Although the [[Hakata Matsubayashi]] (Hakata Dontaku) in May is not strictly a Kushida Shrine festival, the Matsubayashi procession traditionally starts from Kushida Shrine{{Cite book|author=Kiyoshi Tanaka, Osamu Matsuda, Noriko Obayashi, Takashi Tomokiyo |title=Hakata Tabijō: Shiki no Gyoji Rekishi Yomoyama |publisher=Eizo Yagi |date=1967-04-25 |page=88}}{{Cite book|author=Seizo Inoue |title=Hakata Kyōdo Shi Jiten |publisher=Sanda Hisamoto |date=1987-11-01 |pages=264-266}}. == festival Deities == The shrine enshrines three deities: [[Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami]] (Kushida no Ōkami), [[Amaterasu]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Ōkami]] (Gion no Ōkami). The main hall enshrines Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the left hall enshrines Amaterasu Ōmikami, and the right hall enshrines Susanoo no Ōkami. Unlike other Kushida Shrines across Japan that mainly enshrine [[Kushinadahime]], Kushinadahime is not enshrined here, although there is a theory that this shrine originally enshrined her. Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami (Kushida no Ōkami) is believed to be a deity transferred from {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka|1=Kushida}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. Also known as Ōwakako no Mikoto, he is a descendant of [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] and is said to have vanquished monsters in the Hokuriku region. There is a shrine dedicated to Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami in [[Sado City]], and among the five shrines dedicated to Kushinadahime, three are located in the Hokuriku region. == History == According to shrine tradition, Kushida Shrine was established in {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 1 (757) when the deity from {{ill|Kushida |en|Kushida |qid=Q1794549|lt=Kushida Shrine in Matsusaka|1=Kushida}} was transferred here, along with Amaterasu Ōmikami, who served the clan of Ōhata-nushi no Ōkami, the deity of the Matsusaka shrine. In {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō|1=Tengyō}} 4 (941), Ono no Yoshifuru prayed at [[Yasaka Shrine]] in Kyoto to suppress the [[Rebellion of Fujiwara no Sumitomo]] and subsequently transferred Susanoo no Ōkami to this shrine after pacifying the rebellion. However, the most widely accepted theory is that in the late Heian period, [[Taira no Kiyomori]] transferred [[Kushida Shrine (Saga)|Kushida Shrine]] in {{ill|Kanzaki|en|Kanzaki|qid=Q1728232|lt=Kanzaki|1=Kanzaki}}, {{ill|Hizen Province|en|Hizen Province|qid=Q906926|lt=Hizen Province|1=Hizen Province}} (now Kanzaki City, Saga Prefecture) to Hakata, a base for Japan-Song trade. This theory is supported by the 'Hakata Yamakasa Record' compiled by the shrine priests and submitted to the Ministry of Education in {{ill|1965|en|1965|qid=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}} and 'The History of Fukuoka' published by Fukuoka City in {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979|1=1979}}, both of which indicate the shrine is a branch of Kushida Shrine in Kanzaki, Saga Prefecture. The shrine fell into decline during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}},'The location of Kushida Shrine in Hakata seems to have been given to the Jesuits by [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshishige|Ōtomo Yoshishige]], who built a church and a priest's residence there. According to 'History of the Jesuits in Japan' (Vol. 10, Western Kyushu Part 2, pp. 27-31, published September 20, 1979, translated by Kiichi Matsuda and Taro Kawasaki, Chūōkōronsha), there was a dispute between the Jesuits and the people of Hakata regarding the storage of tools used for the Hakata Gion Yamakasa festival.' but in {{ill|1587|en|1587|qid=Q6744|lt=1587|1=1587}} (Tensho 15), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} rebuilt the shrine during the reconstruction of Hakata. Before the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] decree in {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), the shrine was managed by {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingū-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} belonging to {{ill|Tōchō-ji|en|Tōchō-ji|qid=Q3547067|lt=Tōchō-ji|1=Tōchō-ji}} during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. In {{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}} (Meiji 24), it was listed as a [[Prefectural Shrine]]. == Miscellaneous == The name of [[Gionmachi]] in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], is said to derive from Gion no Ōkami enshrined at this shrine[http://www.asocie.jp/reizen/profile/record/oldtown1.html History of Reizen District 1945-2007, Reizen District Union]. A decorated Yamakasa float is permanently displayed in the precincts. Since {{ill|1960|en|1960|qid=Q3754|lt=1960|1=1960}} (Showa 35), {{ill|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|en|Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|qid=Q843686|lt=Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting|1=Kyushu Asahi Broadcasting}} (KBC) has been airing 'Hashire! Yamakasa' as an offering. In addition, near the entrance of the precincts stands a 1,000-year-old ginkgo tree called "Kushida no Ginnan," which is mentioned in the '[[Hakata Iwaiuta]]' and is designated a natural monument of the prefecture. The "Cut-Tree Peony,"'[https://web.archive.org/web/20210419114128/https://www.city.karatsu.lg.jp/manabee/kyoiku/kyoiku/inkai/bunkazai/bunkazaihogo/kirigonobotan.html Prefectural Natural Monument Cut-Tree Peony]'. Karatsu City. Archived from the original on April 19, 2021. associated with [[Hata no Chikahira|Hata no Mikawa no Kami Chikahira]] of the [[Matsuura Clan]], whose peonies decorate the Yamakasa, was transplanted from Hizen Town, Karatsu City, in {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} (Heisei 30){{Cite web|title=Hakata Gion Yamakasa and Peony Connection - Kushida Shrine in Fukuoka Receives Peony from Karatsu|url=https://www.saga-s.co.jp/articles/-/308161|website=Saga Newspaper LiVE|accessdate=2019-12-02|language=ja}}. In {{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30), Typhoon No. 22 caused damage such as toppling an [[:en:Enoki|Enoki]] tree in the precincts{{Cite web|title=Kushida Shrine - Fallen Enoki Tree -
